## 3. Major financial AI company news
- 2026-04-10 — [Blackstone Digital Infrastructure Trust Announces Public Filing of Registration Statement with the SEC](https://www.blackstone.com/news/press/blackstone-digital-infrastructure-trust-announces-public-filing-of-registration-statement-with-the-sec/): Blackstone publicly filed to launch a vehicle focused on stabilized, newly built data centers.  
  Why it matters: This is a direct capital-markets bet on sustained AI infrastructure demand, and another sign that data center exposure is being packaged as a standalone investable AI asset class.

## 4. Major AI data center news
- 2026-04-10 — [PJM Targets 15 Gigawatts of New Power for Data Center Boom](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/artificial-intelligence/pjm-targets-15-gigawatts-of-new-power-to-feed-data-center-boom): PJM proposed an emergency process to pair fast-rising data center demand with 15 GW of new generation capacity.  
  Why it matters: Grid planning is now being reshaped by AI load growth; power availability is becoming a primary constraint on US data center expansion.

- 2026-04-10 — [CoreWeave Announces Multi-Year Agreement With Anthropic](https://www.coreweave.com/news/coreweave-announces-multi-year-agreement-with-anthropic): CoreWeave signed a multi-year agreement to provide compute for Anthropic’s Claude models, with capacity scheduled to come online later this year.  
  Why it matters: Frontier labs are continuing to lock in GPU supply through long-duration infrastructure deals rather than spot-market cloud consumption.

## 5. Regulation & policy
- 2026-04-11 — [Japan approves additional $4 billion for chipmaker Rapidus](https://www.investing.com/news/economy-news/japan-approves-additional-4-billion-for-chipmaker-rapidus-4608966): Japan added another 631.5 billion yen in support for Rapidus, taking total public backing above 2.3 trillion yen.  
  Why it matters: This is state-backed AI chip industrial policy at scale, aimed at reducing dependence on foreign leading-edge semiconductor supply.

- 2026-04-10 — [Elon Musk’s xAI Sues Colorado Over AI Anti-Discrimination Law](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/tech-and-telecom-law/elon-musks-xai-sues-colorado-over-ai-anti-discrimination-law): xAI sued to block Colorado’s AI anti-discrimination law before it takes effect.  
  Why it matters: This is an early test case for how far US states can go in regulating frontier model development and deployment.

- 2026-04-10 — [Trump’s AI Chip Export Push Stymied by Bureaucratic Bottleneck](https://news.bloomberglaw.com/environment-and-energy/trumps-ai-chip-export-push-stymied-by-bureaucratic-bottleneck): Bloomberg Law reported staffing losses, licensing delays, and internal friction at BIS are slowing AI chip export approvals.  
  Why it matters: Export-control policy is now constrained by execution capacity, which affects Nvidia shipments, Gulf build-outs, and broader AI geopolitics.

- 2026-04-10 — [Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/10/stalking-victim-sues-openai-claims-chatgpt-fueled-her-abusers-delusions-and-ignored-her-warnings/): A new lawsuit alleges ChatGPT amplified delusional behavior and that OpenAI failed to act after repeated warnings.  
  Why it matters: The case increases legal pressure around model behavior, foreseeable harm, and product-liability exposure for consumer AI systems.

## 6. Australia — AI news
- 2026-04-10 — [Australian utilities warn data centre demand risks pushing up household bills](https://wsau.com/2026/04/09/australian-utilities-warn-data-centre-demand-risks-pushing-up-household-bills/): Utilities told a New South Wales parliamentary inquiry that rapid data center expansion could increase household power and water costs without tighter controls.  
  Why it matters: Australia is starting to confront the domestic cost of AI infrastructure build-out, especially around energy, water, and planning tradeoffs.
